name: Asparagus Omelet
recipeInstructions:
1. Mince 1 clove garlic.
2. Slice 1/2 lb mushrooms.
3. Beat 4 eggs and lightly beat them.
4. Add 2 tablespoons milk to the eggs.
5. Add 1/2 teaspoon salt.
6. Add either 1/4 teaspoon dried basil, crushed or 3/4 teaspoon fresh basil, minced.
7. Add a dash of freshly ground black pepper.
8. Cut asparagus into 1-inch pieces and cook in boiling water until tender, about 2 to 4 minutes.
9. Drain the asparagus thoroughly.
10. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in an 8-inch skillet.
11. Saute garlic and mushrooms until cooked and moisture has evaporated.
12. Remove from the pan and keep warm.
13. In a small bowl, combine eggs, milk, salt, basil, and pepper.
14. Melt the remaining butter in the skillet until foamy, swirling it around to coat evenly.
15. When hot enough that a drop of water sizzles when dropped into the pan, pour in the egg mixture.
16. Tip the pan so the eggs coat the skillet evenly.
17. As the eggs cook, periodically lift up the cooked edges, tilt the pan, and let uncooked egg run underneath.
18. When the eggs are set but still have a shiny surface, place asparagus and mushrooms on one side.
19. Slide the omelet out of the pan, folding the side without vegetables over the top.
20. Serve immediately.
